title: कालभैरव अष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री कालभैरव अष्टकम् ॥

देवराजसेव्यमानपावनाङ्घ्रिपंकजं
व्यालयज्ञसूत्रमिन्दुशेखरं कृपाकरम्।
नारदादियोगिवृन्दवन्दितं दिगम्बरं
काशिकापुराधिनाथकालभैरवं भजे॥ १॥

भानुकोटिभास्वरं भवाब्धितारकं परं
नीलकण्ठमीप्सितार्थदायकं त्रिलोचनम्।
कालकालमम्बुजाक्षमक्षशूलमक्षरं
काशिकापुराधिनाथकालभैरवं भजे॥ २॥

शूलटंकपाशदण्डपाणिमादिकारणं
श्यामकायमादिदेवमक्षरं निरामयम्।
भीमविक्रमं प्रभुं विचित्रताण्डवप्रियं
काशिकापुराधिनाथकालभैरवं भजे॥ ३॥

भुक्तिमुक्तिदायकं प्रशस्तचारुविग्रहं
भक्तवत्सलं स्थितं समस्तलोकविग्रहम्।
विनिक्वणन्मनोज्ञहेमकिंकिणीलसत्कटिं
काशिकापुराधिनाथकालभैरवं भजे॥ ४॥

धर्मसेतुपालकं त्वधर्ममार्गनाशकं
कर्मपाशमोचकं सुशर्मदायकं विभुम्।
स्वर्णवर्णशेषपाशशोभिताङ्गमण्डलं
काशिकापुराधिनाथकालभैरवं भजे॥ ५॥

रत्नपादुकाप्रभाभिरामपादयुग्मकं
नित्यमद्वितीयमिष्टदैवतं निरंजनम्।
मृत्युदर्पनाशनं करालदंष्ट्रमोक्षणं
काशिकापुराधिनाथकालभैरवं भजे॥ ६॥

अट्टहासभिन्नपद्मजाण्डकोशसंततिं
दृष्टिपातनष्टपापजालमुग्रशासनम्।
अष्टसिद्धिदायकं कपालमालिकाधरं
काशिकापुराधिनाथकालभैरवं भजे॥ ७॥

भूतसंघनायकं विशालकीर्तिदायकं
काशिवासलोकपुण्यपापशोधकं विभुम्।
नीतिमार्गकोविदं पुरातनं जगत्पतिं
काशिकापुराधिनाथकालभैरवं भजे॥ ८॥

कालभैरवाष्टकं पठन्ति ये मनोहरं
ज्ञानमुक्तिसाधनं विचित्रपुण्यवर्धनम्।
शोकमोहदैन्यलोभकोपतापनाशनम्
ते प्रयान्ति कालभैरवाङ्घ्रिसन्निधिं ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति श्री कालभैरव अष्टकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
benefits: Sacred Kalabhairava Ashtakam for devotion and spiritual growth
Invokes blessings of Bhairava in all forms
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and bestows spiritual merit
Ideal for Bhairava festivals and daily worship
description: The Kalabhairava Ashtakam, known in Sanskrit as कालभैरव अष्टकम्, is an eight-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Bhairava. Rooted in Adi Shankaracharya, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to invoke blessings of Bhairava in all forms as well as grant divine protection and auspiciousness. Additionally, it is known to remove sins and bestow spiritual merit. This prayer is traditionally recited on Saturday (Shanivar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Kala Bhairava Ashtami to invoke divine grace. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Kalabhairava Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Kalabhairava Ashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn for Bhairava devotion.

Adi Shankara Kala Bhairav Kashi stotra
Bhairav stotra for fear removal protection

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kalabhairava Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kalabhairava Ashtakam (कालभैरव अष्टकम्) is an eight-verse Sanskrit hymn composed by Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE) in praise of Lord Kala Bhairava — the fierce manifestation of Lord Shiva who serves as the eternal Kotwal (chief guardian) of Kashi (Varanasi). The hymn is among the most powerful and rhythmic stotras in the Hindu devotional corpus, composed in the rare bhujangaprayata metre that creates an intense chanting cadence. It opens with the verse Devaraja Sevyamana Pavanangri Pankajam — He whose pure lotus feet are served by Indra, the king of gods; whose sacred thread is a serpent; who wears the moon as his crest; the giver of compassion; saluted by Narada and the host of yogis; the sky-clad one — I worship that Kala Bhairava, the chief Lord of the city of Kashi. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya stotra-ratnavali; Kashi Bhairava temple tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between this hymn and other Bhairava stotras?
seoFaqs.answer: Among the many Bhairava stotras in the Hindu corpus, the Kalabhairava Ashtakam by Adi Shankara holds a special position for several reasons: (1) it is by Shankaracharya himself — making it doctrinally authoritative across all Hindu sampradayas; (2) it specifically venerates Kala Bhairava of Kashi — the supreme Bhairava form — not the eight Ashta-Bhairavas, the Batuk-Bhairava (child form), or the Svarna-Akarshana-Bhairava (gold-attracting form); (3) it focuses on Bhairava as Kshetrapala of Kashi — the gatekeeper of the moksha-puri; (4) it uniquely presents Bhairava as Bhukti-Mukti-Dayakam (granter of both enjoyment and liberation); (5) it is the most rhythmically powerful Bhairava stotra in the bhujangaprayata metre. Other notable Bhairava stotras include the Bhairava Hridayam (tantric, Rudra Yamala source), Batuka Bhairava Stotram (child form), and Svarna-Akarshana Bhairava Stotram (wealth-form). (Sources: Bhairava stotra tradition; comparative Shaiva textual analysis.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Kala Bhairava and what is his iconography?
seoFaqs.answer: Kala Bhairava is the fierce manifestation of Lord Shiva, born from Shiva's third eye to humble Brahma's arrogance. The Ashtakam describes his iconography in vivid detail across the eight verses: serpent yajnopavita (vyala-yajnasutra), moon on his crown (indu-shekhara), sky-clad (digambara — naked), three-eyed (trilochana), blue-throated (nilakantha), shining with the radiance of crores of suns (bhanu-koti-bhasvara), dark-bodied (shyama-kaya), holder of trident, hammer, noose, and rod (shula-tanka-pasha-danda-pani), wearing a garland of skulls (kapala-malika-dhara), accompanied by his vehicle the dog (shvana), with a slim waist adorned with golden tinkling bells (svarna-kinkini), and feet adorned with jewelled sandals (ratna-paduka). His chosen city is Kashi where he eternally dwells as the protector of all who enter and leave. (Sources: Kalabhairava Ashtakam verses 1-8; Shiva Purana Bhairava Khanda.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Karma-Pasha-Mochana in Bhairav worship?
seoFaqs.answer: The fifth verse of the Kalabhairava Ashtakam declares Bhairava as Karma-Pasha-Mochakam — the liberator (mochaka) from the noose (pasha) of karma. This is one of the most theologically powerful epithets in all of Shaiva theology. The pasha (noose) is the binding cord of karma — the accumulated good and bad deeds of countless lifetimes that bind the jiva to samsara. Most deities can grant relief from specific karmic effects (disease, poverty, obstacles), but Kala Bhairava as the master of Kala (time itself) has the unique power to dissolve the entire karmic noose — releasing the devotee from the very root of samsaric bondage. This is why Kashi is called moksha-puri — Bhairava as Kotwal personally cuts the karma-pasha of those who die in Kashi. The same verse describes him as Dharma-Setu-Palaka (protector of the bridge of dharma) and Adharma-Marga-Nashaka (destroyer of the path of adharma). (Sources: Kalabhairava Ashtakam verse 5; Kashi Khanda.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Bhairava's gaze (drishtipata) destroy sins?
seoFaqs.answer: The seventh verse of the Kalabhairava Ashtakam declares: Drishtipata-Nashta-Papa-Jalam — by the mere fall of his gaze (drishtipata), the entire net of sins (papa-jala) is destroyed (nashta). This is among the most powerful claims in any Hindu stotra. The verse asserts that one does not even need to perform elaborate prayashchitta (penance), or undertake long pilgrimages, or accumulate vast merit through yajnas — a single glance from Kala Bhairava is sufficient to instantly destroy the entire accumulated network of sins from countless lifetimes. The image is dramatic: Bhairava with his Atta-Hasa (great laughter) splits the lotus-egg of Brahma (padmaja-anda-kosha — the cosmos), grants the eight siddhis (Ashta-Siddhi-Dayakam), wears a garland of skulls (Kapala-Malika-Dhara). The verse is the ultimate Bhairava-bhakti pramana — proof that devoted recitation of even a single verse with sincere bhava can liberate the devotee from karmic bondage. (Sources: Kalabhairava Ashtakam verse 7.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Kalabhairava Ashtakam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kalabhairava Ashtakam is most powerfully recited on: Kalabhairava Ashtami / Bhairava Jayanti (Margashirsha Krishna Ashtami — November/December); every Krishnashtami (every dark-fortnight 8th day called Kalashtami); every Saturday (Bhairava's day) and Tuesday; Maha Shivaratri; the night-time Bhairava Kaal between 10pm and midnight. Traditional recitation procedure: face south or east, before a Bhairava murti or picture, light a mustard-oil or sesame-oil deepam (not ghee), offer black sesame seeds, urad dal, red hibiscus flowers, jaggery, and feed street dogs (the shvana — Bhairava's vehicle and form). Recite 1, 8, or 108 times in clear rhythmic chant — the bhujangaprayata metre creates a powerful spiritual vibration when chanted aloud. The Ashtakam is also recited as part of pancha-bhairava puja and Kashi Yatra paddhati. Devotees report direct results within 41 days of daily recitation. (Sources: Kashi Bhairava temple paddhati; Drikpanchang Kalabhairav festival calendar.)
